description: This test case evaluates the system's ability to retrieve and synthesize information from multiple, separate document sections to answer a complex question.
question: How does the design and processing of the RDL based LGA with 600mm x 600mm Fan-Out Panel Level Package (FOPLP) influence package warpage control, solder joint reliability, and yield, and what manufacturing process improvements are recommended to optimize cost and reliability based on the evaluation data?

gold_context_sources.evidence: Wettable flanks are an inherent part of the FOPLP RDL based LGA process. In fact, the entire lead protrudes above the mold compound causing solder wetting to occur on all sides of the The test vehicle attributes for leadframe-free RDL based LGA utilizing 600mm x 600mm FOPLP is shown in Figure 10 and Table 1. Package outline for top and bottom view is shown in Figure 11. ... For the backend process, the panel will be singulated in 4 segments prior to backgrind, followed by backside laminate protection, laser marking, final inspection and shipment. ... Structural silicon was added to minimize FOR for warpage control. FOR with 2 structural silicon was 1.5. Additional cycle of learning to eliminate structural silicon as cost reduction was performed with FOR at 3.5. Requirement for warpage control is +/- 7mm for the 600mm FOPLP. In both evaluations with and without structural die, the warpage was within specification for each critical process read point as shown in Figure 16. ... Visual 1x inspection, cross sections and SEM images were performed to confirm package form and fit. ... During the course of AOI, defects observed in initial samples were mold voids due to insufficient mold material in the 2" mold process, metal short, dry film residue and foreign material. The total package inspected were 11,880 units (3 lots). The average yield for first pass evaluation was > 98%. ... Wettable flanks are designed in on FOPLP RDL based LGA process. The entire lead protrudes above the mold compound enabling solder wicking to occur on all sides of the lead. The leads are slightly inset from the sawn package edge and extend at least 42um above the molded surface, to address the need for optical inspection and increases board level reliability with a stronger joint. ... Package Reliability For package reliability testing, 80 random units from the panel were evaluated. Table 2 summarizes the test condition and SEM images after each read point. The units underwent Precondition (J-STD-020), Temperature Cycle condition C (JESD22-A104, -65C/150C) for 1000 cycles, uHAST Condition A (JESD22-A118, 130C/85%RH, 96 hours) and HTS Condition C (JESD22-A103, 175C, 504 hours). All units passed. ... The 75mm x 250mm singulated strips underwent 100% visual inspection and subsequently to OS electrical test. Average OS test yield was > 99% which passed criteria. The strip was then singulated and underwent another round of automated visual inspection. Rejects were mainly attributed to handling. ... For cost savings, it is recommended to fully maximize die population on the 600mm x 600mm panel. In this evaluation, strip size of 75mm x 250mm was selected as a drop in solution to be compatible with backend test method, which is strip testing for existing QFN packages. Single package testing at backend process would be recommended when entire 600mm x 600mm panel is fully populated. ... The elimination of structural die in subsequent evaluation also affects final cost of the package and material usage. Warpage studies without structural die shows that panel warpage is within process specification tolerance. ... For strip type testing, punch holes along the Fan-Out strip edges needs to be optimized to ensure backend test equipment set up is flawless. Component Level Reliability (CLR) tests were performed on random units. 80 units underwent Pre-Condition (J-STD- 020), TC Condition C, uHAST Condition A and HTS Condition C. All units passed JEDEC specifications. Cross sections at T=0 and at each subsequent CLR tests were performed to review integrity of mold to die interface and mold to Cu layers interface. No delamination observed on each read point.

gold_context_sources.section_title: Fan-out PoP Solder Joint Reliability Investigation by System Power Cycling
gold_context_sources.evidence: The DOE SPLIT OF UNDERFILL PROPERTIES AND CONDITIONS Table I indicated that the system power cycling test revealed the HBPoP lifetime is slightly less than that of Fan out PoP, with a decrease in lifetime of less than 5%. The difference in lifetime is not significantly different, suggesting that the package type does not have a major impact on the system power cycling result. ... When considering board-level reliability without underfill processes, we observed that the sensitivity of Fan-out PoP is higher than that HBPoP. The lifetime of fan-out PoP without underfill process decreases by approximately 50% compared to when it has an underfill process. However, the difference in lifetime for HBPoP with or without an underfill process is minimal, at just a 10% decrease. ... Simultaneously, we conducted a physical failure analysis which revealed that the solder ball crack is located on the package's corner and edge. This finding aligns with our experience in standard board level reliability test. ... The proposed hypothesis suggests that thermal-mechanical stress causes these cracks and leads to non-fully cured underfill being squeezed into them. ... As the package size of application processor SOC becomes larger, the distance between the package edge and CPU/GPU location will increase, additionally, there is a need to integrate more capacitors in the CPU/GPU area. This makes it harder to control underfill curing conditions and causes the process window to become more sensitive. ... Based on the results of curing under 2-times curing process, it seems that the widely acknowledged superior material properties of the underfill, such as higher Tg (glass transition temperature) and lower CTE, may not be the primary factor. Instead, the crucial factor appears to be whether the underfill is fully cured. ... According to the DOE experimental result, PCBA at relatively low humidity could help the underfill achieve the full curing requirement. This finding correlated to system level stress results and showed underfill curing process played a critical role for field reliability.

ground_truth_answer: The design and processing of the RDL based LGA with 600mm x 600mm Fan-Out Panel Level Package (FOPLP) critically influence package warpage control, solder joint reliability, and yield. The incorporation of wettable flanks, where the entire lead protrudes above the mold compound, enables solder wicking on all sides, improving solder joint robustness and facilitating optical inspection. Structural silicon was initially added to minimize the fan-out ratio (FOR) to about 1.5, effectively controlling panel warpage within the specified tolerance of +/- 7mm. Subsequent process learning allowed elimination of structural silicon, increasing FOR to 3.5, yet warpage remained within specification, indicating process robustness and potential cost reduction by eliminating structural silicon. The manufacturing process involves singulating the panel into four segments before backgrind and further processing steps, with rigorous inspection and electrical open/short testing yielding over 98% first pass yield and >99% open/short test yield, demonstrating high production quality and reliability. Package reliability tests including JEDEC standard temperature cycling, uHAST, and HTS showed all tested units passing, confirming mechanical and thermal robustness without delamination at critical interfaces. Defects initially observed such as mold voids, metal shorts, and residues were addressed through process improvements. To optimize cost and reliability, it is recommended to maximize die population on the full 600mm x 600mm panel rather than the strip design, enabling single package backend testing instead of strip testing. Punch hole placement along fan-out strip edges should be optimized to ensure flawless backend test equipment setup. Additionally, underfill curing process control is crucial for field reliability; fully cured underfill substantially improves solder joint lifetime and reduces cracking propensity. Low humidity baking prior to underfill application further enhances curing completeness. Overall, the combination of structural design (wettable flanks, lead protrusion), process controls (warpage management, mold compound selection), and underfill curing optimization leads to improved package reliability, yield, and cost efficiency in the RDL based LGA FOPLP process.
